text.title: TTS Provider Comparison
text.summary: Provider capabilities, voice options, model tiers, rate limits, and cost for OpenAI TTS, ElevenLabs, and Google Cloud TTS.

section.title: OpenAI TTS

rule.statement: Use tts-1 for draft/preview generation; switch to tts-1-hd only for final production assets. tts-1-hd has ~2x latency.
rule.rationale: tts-1-hd quality gain is audible only in final delivery; using it during iteration wastes latency and doubles cost.

section.p: Available voices: alloy (balanced), echo (male), fable (storytelling), onyx (authoritative male), nova (friendly female), shimmer (soft/gentle). Speed range: 0.25-4.0. Output formats: mp3, opus, aac, flac, wav, pcm. Cost: $0.015/1k chars (tts-1), $0.030/1k chars (tts-1-hd). Rate limit: 50 RPM on tier 1. Does NOT support SSML.

example.note: Minimal OpenAI TTS call with voice and model selection.
example.code:
from openai import OpenAI client = OpenAI()
response = client.audio.speech.create( model="tts-1", # tts-1-hd for production voice="nova", # alloy|echo|fable|onyx|nova|shimmer input="Hello world", speed=1.0, # 0.25-4.0 response_format="mp3" # mp3|opus|aac|flac|wav|pcm ) response.stream_to_file("output.mp3")

section.title: ElevenLabs
section.p: Best overall voice quality and cloning. Model: eleven_multilingual_v2. Supports custom voice cloning from audio samples. Agent-friendly via Python SDK. Higher cost than OpenAI; multilingual breadth superior to OpenAI.

section.title: Google Cloud TTS

rule.statement: Route SSML markup (prosody, say-as, breaks) to Google Cloud or Azure TTS only; never pass SSML to OpenAI TTS.
rule.rationale: OpenAI TTS reads SSML tags verbatim as literal text — the listener hears angle brackets and attribute names.

section.p: Voices: 400+ across Standard, Neural2, and Studio tiers. SSML fully supported. Cost scales with voice tier; Studio voices have usage caps. Neural2 voices are recommended default for quality-conscious pipelines.

section.title: Self-hosted alternatives
section.p: Coqui TTS (OSS, Python, GPU recommended) and Kokoro TTS (OSS, ~80MB model, CPU-viable, MIT license) are viable for zero-API-cost scenarios. Quality is lower than cloud providers but acceptable for internal tooling.
